input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` won't update at runtime.**
`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML load time. Switch to `DynamicResource` for any value that can change while the page is visible:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✅ Updates when the dictionary changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes by merging a new dictionary into `Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries`.

**2. Activity restart on config change.**
Android destroys and recreates the Activity when `uiMode` changes. Add `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges` in your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Orientation
        | ConfigChanges.UiMode,   // ← add this
    LaunchMode = LaunchMode.SingleTop)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This tells Android your app handles the dark/light switch itself, avoiding the full restart. You can then listen for the change via `OnConfigurationChanged` or `Application.RequestedThemeChanged` in MAUI to trigger your dictionary swap.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two issues with dark mode in their .NET MAUI Android app:

1. **StaticResource doesn't update at runtime** — This is a common mistake. `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead for theme-switchable colors.

2. **App restarts when Android dark mode toggles** — By default, Android recreates the Activity when the UI mode (dark/light) changes. In .NET MAUI, you need to handle the `configChanges` attribute to prevent the activity from being destroyed and recreated.
